_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to analyse how social, moral or ethical positions are represented across a range of literary texts.

_source.w: We are learning to analyse how social, moral or ethical positions are represented across a range of literary texts.
y: Literature is a powerful vehicle for exploring social, moral and ethical questions — analysing this deepens our engagement.
Understanding how positions are represented — not just what they are — develops our critical and ethical thinking.
This analytical skill prepares us to engage with complex ethical issues in literature and in life.
eg: We analyse how a literary text represents a moral dilemma and positions readers to view it in a particular way.
We compare how different texts represent the same ethical issue — like justice, freedom or responsibility.
We discuss how an author's social position influences the ethical perspectives represented in their work.
We evaluate whether a text explores ethical complexity or presents simplistic moral positions.
We identify how language and literary techniques shape our moral response to characters and events.
We write a critical response examining the ethical positions represented in a literary text.
